title: chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. villarsii
intro: Chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. villarsii, also known as villars' meadow parsley or hairy meadow parsley, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it useful in treating urinary tract issues and reducing inflammation in the body. The health benefits of Chaerophyllum hirsutum subsp. villarsii include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders, such as bloating and gas, and its ability to support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
